events.title: Slow Gas Leak Masked by Ventilation Failure
events.narrative: Gas concentration near TANK-01 climbs gradually while wind speed collapses to near-calm, killing natural dispersion. Neither reading alone would trip an alarm. Partway through the window, night-shift supervisor W-05 enters the Tank Farm without an active gas-zone entry permit — exactly when the gas has nowhere to go.

events.title: Early Bearing Failure on an Overdue-Maintenance Compressor
events.narrative: COMP-01 vibration and discharge temperature both trend upward across the day shift but never cross their single-sensor alarm setpoints. Task MT-01 (bearing inspection & lubrication) has been overdue for six days, and health score declines and does not recover — the signature of accumulating mechanical wear, not sensor noise.

events.title: Overpressure Risk While the Relief Path Is Degraded
events.narrative: TANK-01 pressure and shell temperature both climb with the afternoon heat load but stay under their high-high alarm setpoints. At the same time, VALVE-01 (the relief valve) is mid function-test — cutting effective relief capacity roughly in half — while an active hot-work permit is in force in the same restricted zone.
